# Dazzle Pricing Model — 3-Month Launch Plan

<!-- Generated by scripts/pricing-model.py — do not edit by hand -->

## Pricing Tiers

| | Free | Starter ($19.99/mo) | Pro ($79.99/mo) |
|---|---|---|---|
| CPU hours included | 10 hrs/mo | 750 hrs/mo (~1 always-on stage) | 1500 hrs/mo (~2 always-on stages) |
| CPU overage | — | $0.15/hr | $0.04/hr |
| GPU (2 hrs free trial) | then blocked | then $0.90/hr | then $0.70/hr |
| Resolution | 720p | 720p | 720p |
| Stage limit | 1 | 1 | Unlimited |
| Destinations | Dazzle only | Dazzle + 1 external | Dazzle + 5 external |
| Privacy | Public | Public | Private |
| **Base margin** | cost center | 28% pre-tax ($5.61 on $19.99) | 64% pre-tax ($51.22 on $79.99) |
| **Typical PAYG** | — | ~$13.50/mo (60% of users × avg 25 GPU hrs) | ~$21.36/mo (avg 30% overage stage + 30 GPU hrs) |
| **Typical total/user** | -$0.24/mo | ~$33.49/mo ($19.99 base + $13.50 PAYG) | ~$101.35/mo ($79.99 base + $21.36 PAYG) |
| **Typical blended margin** | — | ~44% (thin base + healthy PAYG) | ~62% (strong base + healthy PAYG) |

Every user gets a **2-hour free GPU trial** (one-time, expires after 1 year). After the trial, free users are blocked; paid users pay per hour at their plan rate. Base subscription covers CPU; GPU is billed separately.

**Billing granularity:** Usage is tracked per-second internally and billed per-minute. Overage is reported to Stripe in hourly increments (ceil of metered minutes / 60).

#### How blended estimates are computed

The "Typical PAYG", "Typical total", and "Blended margin" rows above are estimates, not contractual. They model an average user on each plan:

- **Base margin** = `(plan price − CPU cost) / plan price`. CPU cost = `$112/mo node ÷ 8 stages × (included hrs ÷ 730 hrs/mo)`.[^1]
- **Typical PAYG (Starter)** = `GPU adoption (60%) × avg GPU hrs (25) × Starter GPU rate ($0.90)` = $13.50/mo.[^2]
- **Typical PAYG (Pro)** = CPU overage component + GPU component:
  - CPU overage: `30% of Pro users run 1 extra always-on stage × 730 hrs × $0.04/hr` = $8.76/mo averaged across all Pro users.[^3]
  - GPU: `60% adoption × 30 avg hrs × $0.70/hr` = $12.60/mo.
  - Total Pro PAYG = $8.76 + $12.60 = $21.36/mo.
- **Blended margin** = `(typical total − total cost) / typical total`, where total cost includes actual CPU hours used (not included budget) + GPU hours at blended node cost ($0.30/hr at 2/4 packing).[^4]
- **Free tier cost** = `10 CPU hrs × $0.019/hr + one-time 2-hr GPU trial amortized over 12 months` = $0.24/mo.[^5]

[^1]: Starter CPU cost: $112 ÷ 8 × (750 ÷ 730) = $14.38. Pro: $112 ÷ 8 × (1500 ÷ 730) = $28.77.
[^2]: 0.6 × 25 × 0.9 = 13.50. This assumes 60% of Starter users use GPU at all, and those who do average 25 hrs/mo.
[^3]: The 30% overage rate means ~1 in 3 Pro users runs a 3rd always-on stage. The overage cost ($29.20/mo) is averaged across all Pro users: 0.3 × $29.20 = $8.76/mo.
[^4]: Blended margin uses *actual* infra cost, not the included budget. Starter blended cost = actual CPU ($14.38) + GPU (0.6 × 25 × $0.30) = $18.88. Margin = ($33.49 − $18.88) / $33.49 = 44%.
[^5]: Free tier GPU amortization: $0.60 one-time ÷ 12 months = $0.050/mo. Total: $0.19 + $0.050 = $0.24/mo.

### Grants Model

All usage budgets and metering are modeled as **usage grants** — rows in the `usage_grants` table. Three grant shapes:

| Shape | minutes | rate | expires | Example |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Prepaid** | 120 | $0 | 1 year | GPU signup trial (2 hrs) |
| **Budget** | 45,000 | $0 | period end | Starter monthly CPU (750 hrs) |
| **Metered** | unlimited | $X/hr | never | GPU PAYG @ $0.90/hr |

Grants are consumed FIFO (free first, then cheapest metered). On plan change, old metered grants expire and new ones are created at the new plan's rate. Prepaid grants (trials, promos) survive plan changes and cancellations.

### Strategy: Pro-Led Growth

Starter is priced near break-even as an **acquisition funnel** — $19.99 is impulse-purchase territory. Real margin comes from Pro and usage-based revenue (GPU + CPU overage).

**Why this works:**
- **$20 converts hobbyists to paying users.** At $29 they think about it. At $19.99 they just do it. More users in the funnel → more Pro conversions.
- **Pro's $0.04/hr CPU overage makes scaling cheap.** 1500 included hours covers 2 always-on stages. A 3rd stage costs ~$29.20/mo in overage — still 52% margin at $0.019 cost. Users scale up without hitting walls.
- **Unlimited Pro stages creates open-ended expansion revenue.** No cap means power users grow into the product instead of outgrowing it.
- **Starter's 1-stage limit drives upgrades.** A Starter user wanting a 2nd stage must upgrade to Pro — there's no overage path. Pro at $80 includes 2 always-on stages with cheap $0.04/hr overage for more.
- **GPU revenue is independent of tier margins.** Every GPU hour is billed, so GPU packing density only affects GPU margin — it never erodes the base subscription.

### Why These Prices

Prices are derived from infrastructure costs. Starter targets near break-even (~28% pre-tax). Pro targets 40%+ after-tax margin. GPU and CPU overage provide high-margin incremental revenue.

**Per-stage cost at different packing densities:**

| Stages/node | CPU cost/stage/mo | CPU cost/stage/hr | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| 6 | $18.67 | $0.026 | Conservative — heavy workloads |
| 8 | $14 | $0.019 | 720p baseline |
| 10 | $11.20 | $0.015 | Optimistic — lighter workloads |
| 12 | $9.33 | $0.013 | Aggressive — needs profiling |

| Stages/node | GPU cost/stage/hr | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| 1 (Solo tenant worst case) | $0.60 | |
| 2 (50% pack) | $0.30 | |
| 3 | $0.20 | |
| 4 (Full node) | $0.15 | |

**Target revenue:**
- CPU 720p ($14 cost): $14 / 0.533 = **$30/stage/mo** (53% pre-tax for 40% after-tax)
- GPU 50% pack ($0.30 cost): $0.30 / 0.533 = **$0.64/hr**
- GPU solo ($0.60 cost): $0.60 / 0.533 = **$1.29/hr**

**Pricing decisions:**
- **$19.99 Starter** — covers 750 CPU hrs at $14.38 cost. 28% pre-tax / 21% after-tax — intentionally thin. Starter's job is acquisition, not profit. $19.99 is impulse-purchase for indie devs.
- **$79.99 Pro** — covers 1500 CPU hrs at $28.77 cost. 64% pre-tax / 48% after-tax — this is where margin lives. Pro users who scale to 3+ stages pay $0.04/hr overage (still 52% margin), creating expansion revenue.
- **$0.90/$0.70 GPU (Starter/Pro)** — at 50% GPU pack ($0.30 cost): 67%/57% margin. At solo node ($0.60 cost): 33%/14% margin. Solo-node is below target but every GPU hour is revenue-positive. Packing improves with user growth.
- **$0.15 Starter CPU overage** — $0.019 cost = 87% margin. High rate intentional: it makes Pro upgrade math obvious.
- **$0.04 Pro CPU overage** — $0.019 cost = 52% margin. Cheap enough that power users scale without friction, but still healthy margin.
- **Private stages on Pro** gates a high-value capability (client data, pre-launch content)
- **Unlimited Pro stages** — no artificial cap. Revenue scales with usage via overage.
- **Free tier** is an acquisition funnel — one-time 2 GPU hrs creates the "holy crap" moment with urgency (doesn't renew monthly), 10 CPU hrs runs out mid-month and triggers upgrade

### Upgrade Path

- **Free → Starter ($19.99)**: Driven by hitting the 10-hr CPU cap and wanting external destinations (Twitch/YouTube). $19.99 is an impulse buy.
- **Starter → Pro ($79.99)**: Driven by the 1-stage cap — any user wanting a 2nd stage must upgrade. Pro includes 2 always-on stages, private visibility, 5 destinations, cheaper GPU ($0.70 vs $0.90), and unlimited stages via $0.04/hr overage.
- **Pro → Enterprise**: Future tier (post 3-month launch). Driven by SOC 2, team/org support, SLA, volume committed pricing.

## Infrastructure Cost Basis

### Fixed Costs (~$413/mo — always running)

| Component | Spec | Qty | Monthly Cost |
|---|---|---|---|
| Control plane nodes (cpx21) | 3 shared vCPU / 4 GB | 3 | ~$26 |
| Worker nodes (ccx43) | 16 dedicated vCPU / 64 GB | 2 | ~$224 |
| Load balancers (lb11) | Layer 4 | 2 | ~$7 |
| Hetzner volumes (5Gi PG) | CSI storage | 1 | ~$1 |
| Cloudflare | Pro plan + R2 | 1 | ~$25 |
| Clerk (auth) | Pro plan | 1 | ~$125 |
| Domain + misc |  |  | ~$5 |
| **Total** | | | **~$413/mo** |

### Variable Costs

| Resource | Unit Cost | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| ccx43 worker node | $112/mo ($0.15/hr) | 16 dedicated vCPU / 64 GB RAM |
| CPU stage density | 8 stages/node (720p) | Mix of active and light workloads |
| Effective cost per CPU stage | ~$14/mo (~$0.019/hr) | 8 stages/node at 720p |
| GPU hour (RunPod SECURE) | $0.60/hr | RTX 4090, raw node cost |
| GPU hour (blended) | $0.30/hr | Avg across 2/4 node packing |
| R2 storage | ~$0.01/stage/mo | Negligible |
| Stripe fees | 2.9% + $0.30/txn | |
| Tax rate | 25% on income | C-corp |

### GPU Node Economics (RunPod SECURE)

GPU nodes are ephemeral — spin up on stage activation, drain after 5 min idle. Multi-tenant: up to 4 stages per RTX 4090 node. Scheduler packs stages onto lowest-utilization node first.

| Stages on RTX 4090 | Our cost/stage-hr | Revenue @ $0.90 (Starter) | Revenue @ $0.70 (Pro) | Margin (Starter) | Margin (Pro) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (Solo tenant worst case) | $0.60 | $0.90 | $0.70 | **33%** | **14%** |
| 2 (50% pack) | $0.30 | $0.90 | $0.70 | **67%** | **57%** |
| 3 | $0.20 | $0.90 | $0.70 | **78%** | **71%** |
| 4 (Full node) | $0.15 | $0.90 | $0.70 | **83%** | **79%** |

Solo-node GPU margins are thin at launch but every hour is revenue-positive. Margins improve rapidly as packing density increases with user growth.

#### GPU vs CPU cost comparison

RunPod SECURE RTX 4090 node costs ~$0.60/hr. For reference:

| | GPU (RTX 4090) | CPU (ccx43) |
|---|---|---|
| Node cost | $0.60/hr (~$438/mo) | $112/mo ($0.15/hr) |
| Stages per node | 4 | 8 |
| Cost per stage-hour (full node) | $0.15 | $0.019 |
| Cost per always-on stage-month | $109.50 | $14 |

GPU is ~8x more expensive per stage-hour than CPU, which is why GPU is metered hourly (not always-on) and priced at a significant premium.

### CPU Stage Pod Resources

| Container | CPU Request | CPU Limit | RAM Request | RAM Limit |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Streamer (Chrome + Xvfb) | 500m | 3500m | 2Gi | 14Gi |
| Sidecar (app logic) | 100m | 500m | 128Mi | 512Mi |
| Init (restore) | 100m | 500m | 64Mi | 256Mi |
| **Total** | **700m** | **4500m** | **2.2Gi** | **~15Gi** |

## Per-Plan Margin Analysis

### Starter ($19.99/mo — 750 CPU hrs included, near break-even)

| Scenario | Revenue | Cost | Pre-tax margin | After-tax (25%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| CPU only, no GPU | $19.99 | $14.38 | **28%** | **21%** |
| CPU + 10 GPU hrs (2/4 pack) | $28.99 | $17.38 | **40%** | **30%** |
| CPU + 10 GPU hrs (solo node) | $28.99 | $20.38 | **30%** | **22%** |
| CPU + 50 GPU hrs (2/4 pack) | $64.99 | $29.38 | **55%** | **41%** |
| 2nd stage overage (730 hrs × $0.15) | $129.49 | $28.77 | **78%** | **58%** |

The last row is hypothetical — Starter is capped at 1 stage, so a 2nd stage requires upgrading to Pro. If a user somehow ran overage (e.g. the stage ran past their 750-hr budget), they'd pay $0.15/hr.

### Pro ($80/mo — 1500 CPU hrs included, margin engine)

| Scenario | Revenue | Cost | Pre-tax margin | After-tax (25%) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2 CPU stages, no GPU | $79.99 | $28.77 | **64%** | **48%** |
| 2 CPU + 30 GPU hrs (2/4 pack) | $100.99 | $37.77 | **63%** | **47%** |
| 2 CPU + 30 GPU hrs (solo node) | $100.99 | $46.77 | **54%** | **40%** |
| 3 CPU (1 overage @ $0.04 × 730 hrs) | $109.19 | $42 | **62%** | **46%** |
| 5 CPU (3 overage) | $167.59 | $70 | **58%** | **44%** |
| Heavy: 3 CPU + 100 GPU hrs (2/4 pack) | $179.19 | $72 | **60%** | **45%** |

Every Pro scenario exceeds 40% after-tax. The $0.04/hr overage creates smooth expansion revenue without hitting walls.

### Free Tier

| | |
|---|---|
| Cost per free user | ~$0.24/mo (10 CPU hrs × $0.019 + one-time GPU amortized) |
| Expected conversion rate | 15%-25% to paid within 60 days (higher at $20 price point) |
| Effective CAC via free tier | $0.97-$1.61 per converted user |

Free tier conversion rate is higher than the $29 model because $19.99 is an impulse buy. Effective CAC drops accordingly.

## Scale Economics

Fixed costs: **$413/mo**. Variable cost per CPU stage: ~$14/mo (8/node density). GPU: $0.60/hr raw (improves with packing).

### Revenue per user by plan mix

Assumes 60% GPU adoption (avg 25 hrs Starter, 30 hrs Pro), 30% Pro CPU overage (1 extra stage).

**How each cell is computed:**
- **Plan mix**: 55% Starter / 45% Pro. Free users = 0.5× paid users.
- **MRR** = `(n_starter × $33.49) + (n_pro × $101.35)`, using typical total/user from the Pricing Tiers table.
- **Infra cost** = fixed ($413) + extra CPU nodes beyond the 2 already in fixed costs (`ceil(total_stages / 8) − 2` × $112) + GPU hours (`n × adoption × avg_hrs × $0.30/hr`).
- **Total stages** = Starter users × 1 + Pro users × 2 + Pro overage (30% × 1 extra) + Free users × (10/730).
- **Pre-tax margin** = `(MRR − infra cost) / MRR`.

| Paying users | Free | Starter | Pro | MRR | Infra cost | Pre-tax margin | ARR |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 5 | 2 | 3 | 2 | $303.17 | $437.30 | **-44%** | $4K |
| 10 | 5 | 6 | 4 | $606.34 | $461.60 | **24%** | $7K |
| 25 | 12 | 14 | 11 | $1,584 | $871.40 | **45%** | $19K |
| 50 | 25 | 28 | 22 | $3,167 | $1,554 | **51%** | $38K |
| 100 | 50 | 55 | 45 | $6,403 | $2,920 | **54%** | $77K |
| 250 | 125 | 138 | 112 | $15,973 | $7,015 | **56%** | $192K |
| 500 | 250 | 275 | 225 | $32,014 | $13,842 | **57%** | $384K |

Mix: 55% Starter / 45% Pro. Free: 0.5× paid users. ARPU: ~$64.03/mo blended (base + PAYG).

**Break-even: ~8 paying users.** $100K ARR at ~131 users. $500K ARR at ~651.

### GPU adoption sensitivity (at 50 paying users)

| GPU adoption | MRR | Pre-tax margin |
|---|---|---|
| **30%** (conservative) | $2,840 | **50%** |
| **45%** (moderate) | $3,004 | **50%** |
| **60%** (baseline) | $3,167 | **51%** |

Base subscriptions cover fixed costs on their own at ~20 paying users. GPU revenue is upside, not a dependency.

## Persona Validation

GPU usage scales with **production value**, not persona type. Shaders, particle effects, bloom, raymarching all require GPU. CPU handles DOM, CSS, Canvas 2D, and WebGL geometry. The GPU upgrade path is about content quality.

The CPU budget is sized for 24/7 (750 hrs/mo), but most users don't run 24/7. Event-based, part-time, and development use cases pay the full subscription but use a fraction of the budget. **This underutilization is margin we capture** — it's a core part of the pricing strategy.

### Personas

Each persona is grounded in a specific user, how they discover Dazzle, what their first two weeks look like, and how usage stabilizes. Year-1 estimates assume organic growth only (no paid acquisition).

#### 1. The Claude Code Streamer — "streaming my agent working"

**Who:** Developer (25-35) already using Claude Code or Cursor 4+ hrs/day. Has a Twitch account they barely use. Saw a tweet of someone streaming their AI agent building a project.

**Discovery:** Twitter/X, Hacker News Show HN, stream-examples repo.

**Week 1:** Pastes llms-full.txt into Claude Code. Tells it to build a dashboard showing git activity, task progress, terminal output. Syncs to a stage. Streams to Twitch. Gets 2 viewers. Tweaks the layout. Leaves it running.

**Steady state:** Stream runs whenever they're coding — 15-25 hrs/week. They iterate on the dashboard a few times, add a "currently working on" panel, maybe push events from CI. Some make it a habit; ~40% churn after month 2 when novelty fades.

**llms.txt usage:** Heavy — this is literally who it's for. They paste it every session.

| CPU hrs/mo | GPU hrs/mo | Utilization | Plan | Bill | Effective margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 60 | 0 | 8% CPU | Starter | **$19.99** | 94% |
| 100 | 5 | 13% CPU | Starter | **$24.49** | 86% |

**Year-1 funnel:** 200-500 try it → 50-100 stick (3+ months). Low ARPU but highest volume.

#### 2. The Generative Art Looper — "my shader runs 24/7 on Twitch Art"

**Who:** Creative coder (20-40) writing p5.js, Three.js, or raw GLSL. Has a portfolio site. Uses AI to iterate on shader code. Knows about Twitch's Art category.

**Discovery:** r/creativecoding, creative coding Discord, Twitter #genart.

**Week 1:** Builds a generative piece locally. Syncs to Dazzle CPU stage — realizes fragment shader runs at 3 FPS. Tries GPU tier, hits 30 FPS. Impressed. Connects to Twitch Art.

**Steady state:** Builds 2-3 pieces over a month (4-6 hr creative sessions each). Picks one as their "permanent" stream. Leaves it running 24/7. Active dev: 4-6 hrs/week in bursts. Passive streaming: always-on.

**llms.txt usage:** Moderate — during dev sessions to iterate on content. Once the piece is running, they use CLI directly.

| CPU hrs/mo | GPU hrs/mo | Utilization | Plan | Bill | Effective margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 40 (dev) | 10 (dev) | 5% CPU | Starter | **$28.99** | 87% |
| 730 (24/7) | 50 (streams) | 49% CPU | Pro | **$114.99** | 75% |

**Year-1 funnel:** 30-50 try it → 10-15 stick (3+ months). Tiny segment but high ARPU and great for marketing (visible, shareable content).

#### 3. The Crypto Dashboard — "24/7 trading stream, zero effort"

**Who:** Crypto trader or fintech builder (25-45). Has a trading bot or portfolio tracker. Wants a 24/7 YouTube/Twitch stream showing live market data. Currently would need OBS on a VPS — too much hassle.

**Discovery:** Crypto Twitter. Someone shares a stream of a live Dexscreener dashboard and links Dazzle.

**Week 1:** Builds an HTML dashboard with TradingView embeds, live prices via WebSocket, maybe a chat feed. 8-10 hours total. Syncs to Dazzle, connects to YouTube.

**Steady state:** Stream just runs. Updates the dashboard once every 2-3 weeks — 30 min to 2 hrs per update. Essentially zero active compute beyond the always-on stage. **This is the dream customer:** pays Pro/month, costs ~$14 in compute, files zero support tickets.

**llms.txt usage:** Low — used once during initial setup, then CLI directly.

| CPU hrs/mo | GPU hrs/mo | Utilization | Plan | Bill | Effective margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 730 (24/7) | 0 | 49% CPU | Pro | **$79.99** | 82% |

**Year-1 funnel:** 50-100 try it → 20-30 stick (3+ months). Best unit economics of any persona. High retention because the stream generates followers/revenue for them.

#### 4. The Ai Vtuber Builder — "my character streams 24/7"

**Who:** Builder (20-30) deep in the AI companion/VTuber scene. Tried Open-LLM-VTuber or AIRI locally. Bottleneck: needs a PC running OBS 24/7 with a GPU.

**Discovery:** AI VTuber Discord communities, GitHub (Open-LLM-VTuber, AIRI repos).

**Week 1:** Most complex setup — character model (Live2D/3D), TTS pipeline, LLM for personality, lip sync, web renderer. 20+ hours. Heavy AI assistance. Likely in the Dazzle Discord asking questions.

**Steady state:** If they get it working, it runs 24/7. They spend 3-5 hrs/week tweaking personality, monitoring chat integration, adding features. The 24/7 always-on GPU cost ($$0.70/hr × 730 = $511/mo) is prohibitive, so they split: CPU for the web rendering, GPU only for heavy scenes or dev.

**llms.txt usage:** Heavy during setup — their AI agent needs deep understanding of events, persistence, and content sync.

| CPU hrs/mo | GPU hrs/mo | Utilization | Plan | Bill | Effective margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 730 (24/7) | 20 | 49% CPU | Pro | **$93.99** | 79% |
| 730 (24/7) | 30 | 49% CPU | Pro | **$100.99** | 77% |

**Year-1 funnel:** 5-15 try it → 3-5 stick (3+ months). Tiny volume but high ARPU and extremely vocal — they create visible content that attracts more users. Partnership with AIRI/Open-LLM-VTuber could 10x this segment.

#### 5. The Build In Public — "watch my AI build my startup"

**Who:** Solo founder (25-40) building a SaaS. Active on Indie Hackers and Twitter. "Build in public" is their marketing strategy. Streaming their AI agent working is the ultimate form.

**Discovery:** Indie Hackers, Twitter build-in-public community, another "watch me build" stream.

**Week 1:** Sets up a simple status page — current task, recent commits, maybe a terminal-like view. 3-4 hours. Streams to Twitch or YouTube.

**Steady state:** Turns stream on for build sessions — 3-4 sessions/week, 2-4 hrs each. 8-16 hrs/week. Content quality varies wildly (sometimes interesting, sometimes watching CI for 20 minutes). **Novelty wears off fast.** Most stream less by month 2, then cancel.

**llms.txt usage:** Moderate — pasted at start of build sessions.

| CPU hrs/mo | GPU hrs/mo | Utilization | Plan | Bill | Effective margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 30 | 0 (trial) | 100% CPU | Free | **$0** | — |
| 60 | 2 (trial) | 8% CPU | Starter | **$21.79** | 92% |

**Year-1 funnel:** 300-500 try it → 30-50 stick (3+ months). Largest top-of-funnel but highest churn. Most stay on Free or barely use Starter.

### Summary

| Persona | Try (yr 1) | Stick (3+ mo) | Monthly ARPU | Monthly rev | Best margin driver |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code Streamer | 200-500 | 50-100 | $19.99-$24.49 | $999.50-$2,449 | Volume + low utilization |
| Generative Art Looper | 30-50 | 10-15 | $28.99-$114.99 | $289.90-$1,725 | GPU PAYG + marketing value |
| Crypto Dashboard | 50-100 | 20-30 | $79.99 | $1,600-$2,400 | Best unit economics (set-and-forget) |
| Ai Vtuber Builder | 5-15 | 3-5 | $93.99-$100.99 | $281.97-$504.95 | High ARPU + community growth |
| Build In Public | 300-500 | 30-50 | $0-$21.79 | $0-$1,090 | Top-of-funnel only |
| **Total** | **585-1165** | **113-200** | **~$34.45 blended** | **$3,171-$8,168** | |

**Organic year-1 estimate: $38.05K-$98.02K ARR.** Not $500K. The path to $500K requires either (a) a partnership that makes Dazzle the default streaming backend for a framework (AIRI, Browser Use), or (b) an API/platform tier where other products build on top of Dazzle stages.

### Key dynamics

- **Crypto dashboard operators are the best unit economics.** They pay $80/month, cost $14, barely touch the product after setup, and don't churn because the stream generates value for them.
- **Claude Code streamers are the volume play** but low ARPU ($19.99/mo) and churn-prone. The question is whether "streaming your agent" becomes a *habit* or a *novelty*.
- **Generative art loopers are high-value but tiny.** Great for marketing — visible, shareable content — but the addressable market is 30-50 people.
- **AI VTubers are the dream but setup cost is brutal.** A framework partnership (AIRI, Open-LLM-VTuber) that bundles Dazzle as the default streaming backend would change the economics entirely.
- **"Build in public" is a trap.** Huge top-of-funnel, terrible retention. Content quality is too inconsistent to keep viewers, so there's no feedback loop keeping the streamer engaged.
- **Underutilization is the long tail.** Claude Code streamers pay $19.99 for 750hrs but use 60 — CPU cost is ~$1.15, not $14.38. Build-in-public users pay $19.99 for 30hrs — cost is ~$0.58. This is how gym memberships work.
- **The 1-stage cap works**: Starter users wanting a 2nd stage must upgrade to Pro ($79.99). No overage workaround — the limit is hard.
- **The missing persona is the platform builder** — someone building *on top of* Dazzle (a SaaS using stages as infrastructure). That's the path to $500K ARR, but it's an API customer, not an llms.txt user.

## Market Sizing (TAM / SAM / SOM)

Bottom-up market sizing grounded in verified competitor data and persona analysis. Top-down analyst figures provided for context but not used as inputs — they're too broad to be actionable.

### TAM — Total Addressable Market

The total spend across markets where Dazzle's technology is relevant. **$126.20B** combined in 2026 — but Dazzle doesn't compete for most of this.

| Market | 2026 Size | CAGR | 2029 Projected | Source | Relevance to Dazzle |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Live streaming platforms & tools | $97.0B | 27% | $198.7B | Mordor Intelligence | Includes Twitch, YouTube Live, Kick, Restream, StreamYard, OBS ecosystem |
| AI agent infrastructure | $11.8B | 41% | $33.1B | Fortune Business Insights | Browserbase, E2B, Steel, Hyperbrowser + broader agent tooling |
| Digital signage software | $14.3B | 12% | $20.1B | The Business Research Company | ScreenCloud, Yodeck, OptiSigns — programmatic display content |
| VTuber / virtual creator economy | $3.1B | 10% | $4.1B | Mordor Intelligence | Live2D, AI companions, avatar streaming |
| **Total TAM** | **$126.2B** | | **$256B** | | |

**AI agent infrastructure is the fastest-growing TAM segment** at 41% CAGR — from $11.8B (2026) to $33B (2029). This is the tailwind Dazzle rides: as more agents are deployed, more need a way to show their work visually.

The TAM is large but misleading. Dazzle doesn't compete with Twitch itself or enterprise signage hardware — it competes for a thin slice: **developers and creators who want programmatic, always-on streaming without managing infrastructure.**

### SAM — Serviceable Addressable Market

Segments where Dazzle directly competes or creates a new category. **~$100M** in annual spend (2026).

| Segment | 2026 Spend | CAGR | 2029 Projected | Dazzle's angle |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud browser-as-a-service | ~$50M | 80% | ~$292M | Dazzle adds streaming output — no competitor does browser + broadcast |
| 24/7 streaming automation | ~$30M | 15% | ~$46M | Competitors loop pre-recorded video; Dazzle runs live, dynamic content |
| AI VTuber infrastructure | ~$5M | 100% | ~$40M | Every framework requires local OBS + GPU; Dazzle eliminates both |
| Developer streaming / build-in-public | ~$10M | 50% | ~$34M | AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor) create new content type |
| Creative coding / generative art streaming | ~$5M | 20% | ~$9M | GPU stages run shaders at 30 FPS without local hardware |
| **Total SAM** | **~$100M** | | **~$420M** | |

**The SAM nearly 4x's by 2029** — from ~$100M to ~$420M. The fastest-growing segment is AI VTuber infrastructure (100% CAGR), driven by GitHub star counts: AIRI 34K, Open-LLM-VTuber 6.2K, AI-Vtuber 4.3K.

**Key insight:** The SAM is small today (~$100M) because these are early, fragmented markets. Cloud browsers (~$50M) is the largest segment but Dazzle differentiates by adding streaming output — no competitor does browser + broadcast. The 24/7 streaming automation segment (~$30M) is the most direct competitor space, but incumbents loop pre-recorded video while Dazzle runs live content.

#### SAM in customers (not just dollars)

The SAM contains an estimated **28K-54K active customers/builders** today. Not all are reachable — most cloud browser users don't need streaming, most 24/7 streamers want simple video loops. The "capturable" column estimates what % would switch to or add Dazzle.

| Segment | Active customers (2026) | Basis | Capturable for Dazzle | Capturable count |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Cloud browser-as-a-service | 5,000-8,000 | Browserbase 1K+ paying orgs + 20K devs (Contrary Research). E2B, Steel, Hyperbrowser est. 4K-7K combined. | 2% | 100-160 |
| 24/7 streaming automation | 15,000-25,000 | LiveReacting: 10K+ creators (livereacting.com). Gyre, OhBubble, Restream 24/7 segment est. 5K-15K. | 1% | 150-250 |
| AI VTuber infrastructure | 500-1,500 | GitHub: AIRI 34K stars, Open-LLM-VTuber 6.2K, AI-Vtuber 4.3K, plus ~8 smaller frameworks. Active builders est. 1-3% of stargazers. | 3% | 15-45 |
| Developer streaming / build-in-public | 5,000-15,000 | Twitch Science & Technology: est. 3K-5K active monthly streamers. AI coding agent users who stream: Claude Code ~18.9M MAU, Cursor 360K+ subscribers — even 0.01% = 2K-5K potential streamers. | 2% | 100-300 |
| Creative coding / generative art streaming | 2,000-5,000 | Twitch Art category: 4.1M followers, est. 2K-5K monthly active streamers. Generative art subset (code-based, not painting/drawing) est. 500-1,500. | 2% | 40-100 |
| **Total** | **28K-54K** | | | **405-855** |

**405-855 capturable customers** aligns with the persona-based SOM of 113-200 year-1 users — the bottom-up (persona funnels) and top-down (SAM penetration) estimates converge, which increases confidence in the range.

#### Rollout model: Year 1-2 customer acquisition

Quarter-by-quarter projection grounded in launch sequence (directories → communities → partnerships):

| Quarter | New paying users | Cumulative (est.) | Acquisition channel | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Q1 (launch) | 15-30 | 15-30 | Directories (TAAFT, Toolify) + Show HN + r/SideProject | Early adopters from AI/dev communities. Mostly Claude Code streamers + build-in-public. |
| Q2 | 25-50 | 38-77 | Word-of-mouth + Reddit (r/creativecoding, r/AI_Agents) + Product Hunt | Creative coders discover GPU stages. First crypto dashboard operators. Churn stabilizes. |
| Q3 | 35-60 | 69-129 | SEO matures + first framework partnership (AIRI or Browser Use) | Partnership drives VTuber builders. Crypto dashboards grow via visible streams. |
| Q4 | 40-70 | 102-186 | Second partnership + newsletter features + organic compound | Pro tier adoption increases as 24/7 users discover overage trap. ARPU shifts up. |
| Q5 (Y2Q1) | 50-90 | 141-257 | Framework integrations live + API/platform tier attracts first SaaS builder | Platform customer could equal 20-50 individual users in stage count. |
| Q6 | 55-95 | 181-326 | Organic + partnerships compound | SAM itself has grown ~40% from launch. More cloud browser users discover streaming use case. |
| Q7 | 55-95 | 217-388 | Steady state organic + 2-3 active partnerships | Retention improves as product matures. GPU packing density improves margins. |
| Q8 (Y2Q4) | 60-100 | 255-449 | Enterprise tier early access + API customers | First enterprise deal could add $2K-5K MRR from single customer. |

**Assumes 10% quarterly churn** on the cumulative base (new users added after churn is applied). This is conservative — crypto dashboard operators and 24/7 streamers churn much less, while build-in-public users churn much more.

**Year-end snapshots:** Year 1: 102-186 paying users. Year 2: 255-449 paying users.

**ARR at year-end** (at $64.03/mo blended ARPU[^6]): Year 1: $78.37K-$142.91K. Year 2: $195.92K-$344.98K.

[^6]: Blended ARPU = `55% Starter × $33.49 + 45% Pro × $101.35` = $64.03/mo. This is the scale economics ARPU including typical PAYG (GPU + overage). Early quarters skew lower (more Free/Starter), later quarters skew higher (more Pro + partnerships).

#### Platform context

- **7.1M** unique Twitch channels went live monthly (2026)
- **97.2K** average concurrent Twitch channels
- **25.8K** concurrent viewers in Twitch/Kick crypto categories
- **95%** of developers use AI coding tools weekly
- **46%** of developers rate Claude Code as "most loved" AI coding tool
- **Browserbase**: $4.4M ARR, 1,000+ paying customers, $300M valuation (68x ARR) — validates cloud browser demand
- **three.js**: 108K GitHub stars — large creative coding ecosystem, but most creators work locally

### SOM — Serviceable Obtainable Market

What Dazzle can realistically capture, derived bottom-up from persona analysis (see Persona Validation).

| | Year 1 (organic) | Year 2 (organic + partnerships) |
|---|---|---|
| Paying users | 113-200 | 339-600 |
| Blended ARPU | ~$28.06-$40.84/mo | ~$28.06-$40.84/mo (shifts toward Pro) |
| ARR | $38.05K-$98.02K | $114.16K-$294.05K |
| SAM penetration | 0.10% | 0.3% |

#### Year-1 breakdown by persona

| Persona | Stick users | ARPU | Monthly rev | Annual rev |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code Streamer | 50-100 | $19.99-$24.49 | $999.50-$2,449 | $11,994-$29,388 |
| Generative Art Looper | 10-15 | $28.99-$114.99 | $289.90-$1,725 | $3,479-$20,698 |
| Crypto Dashboard | 20-30 | $79.99 | $1,600-$2,400 | $19,198-$28,796 |
| Ai Vtuber Builder | 3-5 | $93.99-$100.99 | $281.97-$504.95 | $3,384-$6,059 |
| Build In Public | 30-50 | $0-$21.79 | $0-$1,090 | $0-$13,074 |
| **Total** | **113-200** | | | **$38,054-$98,016** |

#### Year-2 growth assumptions

Year 2 assumes 3x user growth from:
- **1-2 framework partnerships** (AIRI, Open-LLM-VTuber, or Browser Use) that bundle Dazzle as default streaming backend
- **Word-of-mouth** from visible streams (generative art, crypto dashboards attract organic discovery)
- **SEO/directory presence** established in year 1 (Product Hunt, HN, AI directories)
- **No paid acquisition assumed** — partnerships and organic only

#### Upside scenario: Platform/API tier

The SOM above covers individual users. The path to $500K+ ARR requires **platform customers** — companies building on top of Dazzle stages as infrastructure (digital signage SaaS, AI streaming platforms, interactive broadcast tools). One platform customer with 50-100 stages could equal 50+ individual users. This segment isn't modeled in the persona analysis because it requires an API/enterprise tier that doesn't exist yet.

### TAM → SAM → SOM Summary

```
TAM  $126B → $256B (2029)    Adjacent markets
  ↓
SAM  ~$100M → ~$420M (2029)   Segments where Dazzle competes
  ↓
SOM  $38K-$98K   Year 1 (organic, 113-200 users)
     $114K-$294K  Year 2 (organic + partnerships, 339-600 users)
```

**SAM penetration at year 2: 0.3%.** But the SAM is a moving target — it grows to ~$420M by 2029. Cloud browsers alone grew from ~$0 to ~$50M in 18 months (Browserbase: $0 → $4.4M ARR in 16 months). Dazzle's streaming differentiation could expand the SAM further by creating demand that doesn't exist yet (agent broadcasting, programmatic live streams).

## Long-Term Scale (With Future Enterprise Tier)

Enterprise tier ($40/stage committed, $0.45/hr GPU) is the long-term margin engine. Not included in the 3-month plan — prerequisites:

- SOC 2 Type II (~3-6 months, ~$20-50K)
- Team/org support with RBAC
- SLA with uptime guarantee (99.9%)
- Audit logging
- Dedicated capacity / namespace isolation

| Scale | Paying Users | Revenue/mo | Infra Cost/mo | Net (after tax)/mo | Net Margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 14 | 14 | $980.09 | $600.60 | $284.62 | 29% |
| 50 | 50 | $3,469 | $1,689 | $1,335 | 38% |
| 100 | 100 | $6,870 | $3,076 | $2,845 | 41% |
| 500 | 500 | $34,352 | $14,514 | $14,878 | 43% |
| 1000 | 1000 | $68,703 | $28,728 | $29,981 | 44% |

(Assumes 45% Starter / 35% Pro / 20% Enterprise mix at maturity, with GPU and overage usage scaling proportionally.)

**Key milestones:** $100K ARR at ~121 users. $500K ARR at ~607 users. $1M ARR at ~1214 users.

## What Needs to Be Built

### Phase 1: Paid Launch (~4-6 weeks)

1. **Stripe Integration** (~1-2 weeks)
   - Stripe Checkout for plan signup
   - Stripe Billing for metered usage (GPU hours + CPU overage)
   - Webhook handler (subscription lifecycle events)
   - New DB: `subscriptions` table

2. **Usage Metering** (~1 week)
   - Track stage active duration (`activated_at` / `deactivated_at`)
   - Track CPU hours consumed per user per billing period
   - Track GPU hours consumed per user per billing period
   - New DB: `usage_events` table
   - Hourly rollup job reporting to Stripe Billing Meters (CPU + GPU reported independently)

3. **Plan Enforcement** (~3-5 days)
   - New `plan` column on users table (free/starter/pro)
   - Free tier: enforce 10 CPU hrs/mo budget + one-time 2 GPU hr trial
   - Starter: 1 stage limit, 750 CPU hrs/mo, $0.15/hr CPU overage, $0.90/hr GPU
   - Pro: unlimited stages, 1500 CPU hrs/mo, $0.04/hr CPU overage, $0.70/hr GPU
   - Destination limit per plan (free: 0 external, starter: 2, pro: 5)

4. **Stage Privacy** (~2-3 days)
   - New `visibility` column on stages: `public` or `private`
   - Modify ListStages to respect visibility
   - Modify HLS proxy to check auth for private stages
   - Free/Starter: public only. Pro: private option.

5. **Billing UI** (~1 week)
   - Plan selection / upgrade page
   - Current usage display (CPU hrs used vs 750/1500 included, GPU hrs + cost this period)
   - Overage rate shown per plan (Starter users see the Pro upgrade math)
   - Stripe Customer Portal link (payment method, invoices)
   - Plan badge in navigation

### Already Built (Leveraged for Billing)

- Per-user quotas: `max_stages`, `max_active_cpu_stages`, `max_active_gpu_stages`
- GPU vs CPU tier differentiation (separate providers, pod specs, encoding)
- Resolution configurable via `SCREEN_WIDTH`/`SCREEN_HEIGHT` env vars
- External destination limit (hardcoded to 3 — change to per-plan)
- Stage lifecycle state machine
- API key auth (Clerk JWT + `dzl_` API keys)

### Phase 2: Post-Launch Improvements (~4-6 weeks)

- Annual billing discount (2 months free = 17% discount)
- Webhook notifications (stage health, stream drops)
- SLA + status page
- GPU idle drain window extension (5 min → 15-20 min)
- GPU prepaid packs (buy 50 hrs at discount, no expiry)

### Phase 3: Enterprise Tier (~3-6 months)

- SOC 2 Type II
- Team/org support with RBAC
- Audit logging
- Volume committed pricing ($40/stage)
- Dedicated capacity / namespace isolation
- Custom domains / white-label

### SAM CAGR methodology

SAM segment CAGRs are estimates, not analyst-sourced figures. Methodology:

| Segment | CAGR | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| Cloud browsers (80%) | Browserbase grew from $0 to $4.4M ARR in 16 months; category has 4+ funded startups (Browserbase, E2B, Steel, Hyperbrowser). 80% reflects early-stage hypergrowth that will decelerate. |
| 24/7 streaming (15%) | Mature segment (LiveReacting est. 2018, Gyre est. 2020). Growth tracks overall live streaming market (~27%) but discounted for incumbency. |
| AI VTuber (100%) | Nascent market — AIRI grew from 0 to 34K GitHub stars in ~12 months. Open-LLM-VTuber similar trajectory. Doubling annually is conservative for a new category. |
| Dev streaming (50%) | Driven by AI coding tool adoption (95% of devs weekly, up from ~40% in 2024). New content type that didn't exist before Claude Code/Cursor. |
| Creative coding (20%) | Stable niche growing with WebGL ecosystem (three.js 108K stars). Growth tracks broader creative tools market. |
